author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XII. 46-49.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “While He yet talked to the people, behold, His mother and His brethren stood without, desiring to speak with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then one said unto Him, Behold, Thy mother and Thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He answered and said unto him that told Him, Who is my mother, andmy brethren?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He stretched forth His hand towards His disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That which I was lately saying, that when virtue is wanting all things are vain, this is now also pointed out very abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I indeed was saying, that age and nature, and to dwell in the wilderness, and all such things, are alike unprofitable, where there is not a good mind; but to-day we learn in addition another thing, that even to have borne Christ in the womb, and to have brought forth that marvellous birth, hath no profit, if there be not virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is hence especially manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For while He yet talked to the people,” it is said, “one told Him, Thy mother and Thy brethren seek Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He saith, who is my mother, and who are my brethren?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He said, not as being ashamed of His mother, nor denying her that bare Him; for if He had been ashamed of her, He would not have passed through that womb; but as declaring that she hath no advantage from this, unless she do all that is required to be done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in fact that which she had essayed to do, was of superfluous vanity; in that she wanted to show the people that she hath power and authority over her Son, imagining not as yet anything great concerning Him; whence also her unseasonable approach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at all events both her self-confidenceand theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since when they ought to have gone in, and listened with the multitude; or if they were not so minded, to have waited for His bringing His discourse to an end, and then to have come near; they call Him out, and do this before all, evincing a superfluous vanity, and wishing to make it appear, that with much authority they enjoin Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this too the evangelist shows that he is blaming, for with this very allusion did he thus express himself, “While He yet talked to the people;” as if he should say, What? was there no other opportunity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, was it not possible to speak with Him in private?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what was it they wished to say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if it were touching the doctrines of the truth, they ought to have propounded these things publicly, and stated them before all, that the rest also might have the benefit: but if about other matters that concerned themselves, they ought not to have been so urgent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He suffered not the burial of a father, lest the attendance on Him should be interrupted, much less ought they to have stopped His discourse to the people, for things that were of no importance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is clear, that nothing but vainglory led them to do this; which John too declares, by saying, “Neither did His brethren believe on Him;”and some sayings too of theirs he reports, full of great folly; telling us that they were for dragging Him to Jerusalem, for no other purpose, but that they themselves might reap glory from His miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if thou do these things,” it is said, “show Thyself to the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is no man that doeth anything in secret, and seeketh himself to be manifest;”when also He Himself rebuked them, attributing it to their carnal mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, because the Jews were reproaching Him, and saying, “Is not this the carpenter’s son, whose father and mother we know? and His brethren, are not they with us?”they, willing to throw off the disparagement caused by His birth, were calling Him to the display of His miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause He quite repels them, being minded to heal their infirmity; since surely, had it been His will to deny His mother, He would have denied her then, when the Jews were reproaching Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as it is, we see that He takes so great care of her, as even at the very cross to commit her to the disciple whom He loved most of all, and to give him a great charge concerning her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now He doth not so, out of care for her, and for His brethren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, because their regard for Him was as towards a mere man, and they were vainglorious, He casts out the disease, not insulting, but correcting them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou, I pray, examine not the words only, which contain a moderate reproof, but also the unbecoming conduct of His brethren, and the boldness wherewith they had been bold and who was the person reproving it, no mere man, but the only-begotten Son of God; and with what purpose He reproved; that it was not with intent to drive them to perplexity, but to deliver them from the most tyrannical passion and to lead them on by little and little to the right idea concerning Himself, and to convince her that He was not her Son only, but also her Lord: so wilt thou perceive that the reproof is in the highest degree both becoming Him and profitable to her, and withal having in it much gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “Go thy way, tell my mother, thou art not my mother,” but He addresses Himself to the person that told Him; saying, “Who is my mother?” together with the things that have been mentioned providing for another object also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That neither they nor others confiding in their kindred, should neglect virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if she is nothing profited by being His mother, were it not for that quality in her, hardly will any one else be saved by his kindred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is one only nobleness, to do the will of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This kind of noble birth is better than the other, and more real.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing therefore these things, let us neither pride ourselves on children that are of good report, unless we have their virtue; nor upon noble fathers, unless we be like them in disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is possible, both that he who begat a man should not be his father, and that he who did not beget him should be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore in another place also, when some woman had said, “Blessed is the womb that bare Thee, and the paps which Thou hast sucked;” He said not, “The womb bare me not, neither did I suck the paps,” but this, “Yea rather, blessed are they that do the will of my Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how on every occasion He denies not the affinity by nature, but adds that by virtue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And His forerunner too, in saying, “O generation of vipers, think not to say, We have Abraham to our father,”means not this, that they were not naturally of Abraham, but that it profits them nothing to be of Abraham, unless they had the affinity by character; which Christ also declared, when He said, “If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham;”not depriving them of their kindred according to the flesh, but teaching them to seek after that affinity which is greater than it, and more real.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then He establishes here also, but in a manner less invidious, and more measured, as became Him speaking to His mother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not at all, “She is not my mother, nor are those my brethren, because they do not my will;” neither did He declare and pronounce judgment against them; but He yet left in it their own power to choose, speaking with the gentleness that becomes Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For he that doeth,” saith He, “the will of my Father, this is my brother, and sister, and mother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore if they desire to be such, let them come this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when the woman again cried out, saying, “Blessed is the womb that bare Thee,” He said not, “She is not my mother,” but, “If she wishes to be blessed, let her do the will of my Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such a one is both brother, and sister, and mother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh honor! oh virtue!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Unto what a height doth she lead up him that follows after her!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How many women have blessed that holy Virgin, and her womb, and prayed that they might become such mothers, and give up all!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is there to hinder?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For behold, He hath marked out a spacious road for us; and it is granted not to women only, but to men also, to be of this rank, or rather of one yet far higher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this makes one His mother much more, than those pangs did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if that were a subject for blessing, much more this, inasmuch as it is also more real.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not therefore merely desire, but also in the way that leads thee to thy desire walk thou with much diligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then said these words, “He came out of the house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how He both rebuked them, and did what they desired?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which He did also at the marriage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there too He at once reproved her asking unseasonably, and nevertheless did not gainsay her; by the former correcting her weakness, by the latter showing His kindly feeling toward His mother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So likewise on this occasion too, He both healed the disease of vainglory, and rendered the due honor to His mother, even though her request was unseasonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “in the same day,” it is said, “went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if ye desire, saith He, to see and hear, behold I come forth and discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus having wrought many miracles, He affords again the benefit of His doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He “sits by the sea,” fishing and getting into His net them that are on the land.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He “sat by the sea,” not without a purpose; and this very thing the evangelist has darkly expressed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to indicate that the cause of His doing this was a desire to order His auditory with exactness, and to leave no one behind His back, but to have all face to face,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And great multitudes,” saith He, “were gathered together unto Him, so that He went into a ship and sat, and the whole multitude stood on the shore.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And having sat down there, He speaks by parables.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He spake,” it says, “many things unto them in parables.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet on the mount, we know, He did no such thing, neither did He weave His discourse with so many parables, for then there were multitudes only, and a simple people; but here are also Scribes and Pharisees.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou mark, I pray thee, what kind of parable He speaks first, and how Matthew puts them in their order.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which then doth He speak first?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That which it was most necessary to speak first, that which makes the hearer more attentive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because He was to discourse unto them in dark sayings, He thoroughly rouses His hearers’ mind first by His parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also another evangelist saith that He reproved them, because they do not understand; saying, “How knew ye not the parable?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not for this cause only doth He speak in parables, but that He may also make His discourse more vivid, and fix the memory of it in them more perfectly, and bring the things before their sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In like manner do the prophets also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the parable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Behold,” saith He, “a sowerwent forth to sow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence went He forth, who is present everywhere, who fills all things? or how went He forth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not in place, but in condition and dispensation to usward, coming nearer to us by His clothing Himself with flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because we could not enter, our sins fencing us out from the entrance, He comes forth unto us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore came He forth? to destroy the ground teeming with thorns? to take vengeance upon the husbandmen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means; but to till and tend it, and to sow the word of godliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by seed here He means His doctrine, and by land, the souls of men, and by the sower, Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then comes of this seed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Three parts perish, and one is saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when He sowed, some seeds fell,” He saith, “by the way side; and the fowls came and devoured them up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, that He cast them, but that “they fell.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And some upon the rock, where they had not much earth; and forthwith they sprang up, because they had no deepness of earth; and when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And some fell among the thorns, and the thorns sprang up, and choked them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But others fell on the good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath ears to hear let him hear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A fourth part is saved; and not this all alike, but even here great is the difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now these things He said, manifesting that He discoursed to all without grudging.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as the sower makes no distinction in the land submitted to him, but simply and indifferently casts his seed; so He Himself too makes no distinction of rich and poor, of wise and unwise, of slothful or diligent, of brave or cowardly; but He discourses unto all, fulfilling His part, although foreknowing the results; that it may be in His power to say, “What ought I to have done, that I have not done?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the prophets speak of the people as of a vine; “For my beloved,” it is said, “had a vineyard;”and, “He brought a vine out of Egypt;”but He, as of seed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What could this be to show?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That obedience now will be quick and easier, and will presently yield its fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when thou hearest, “The sower went forth to sow,” think it not a needless repetition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the sower frequently goes forth for some other act also, either to plough, or to cut out the evil herbs, or to pluck up thorns, or to attend to some such matter; but He went forth to sow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then, tell me, was the greater part of the seed lost?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not through the sower, but through the ground that received it; that is, the soul that did not hearken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore doth He not say, Some the careless received, and lost it; some the rich, and choked it, and some the superficial, and betrayed it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not His will to rebuke them severely, lest He should cast them into despair, but He leaves the reproof to the conscience of His hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this was not the case with the seed only, but also with the net; for that too produced many that were unprofitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this parable He speaks, as anointing His disciples, and to teach them, that even though the lost be more than such as receive the word yet they are not to despond.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this was the ease even with their Lord, and He who fully foreknew that these things should be, did not desist from sowing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how can it be reasonable, saith one, to sow among the thorns, on the rock, on the wayside?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With regard to the seeds and the earth it cannot be reasonable; but in the case of men’s souls and their instructions, it hath its praise, and that abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the husbandman indeed would reasonably be blamed for doing this; it being impossible for the rock to become earth, or the wayside not to be a wayside, or the thorns, thorns; but in the things that have reason it is not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is such a thing as the rock changing, and becoming rich land; and the wayside being no longer trampled on, nor lying open to all that pass by, but that it may be a fertile field; and the thorns may be destroyed, and the seed enjoy full security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For had it been impossible, this Sower would not have sown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if the change did not take place in all, this is no fault of the Sower, but of them who are unwilling to be changed: He having done His part: and if they betrayed what they received of Him, He is blameless, the exhibitor of such love to man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou mark this, I pray thee; that the way of destruction is not one only, but there are differing ones, and wide apart from one another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they that are like the wayside are the coarse-minded,and indifferent, and careless; but those on the rock such as fail from weakness only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “that which is sown upon the stony places,” saith He, “the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while; but when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When any one,” so He saith, “heareth the word of truth and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth that which was sown out of his heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is he that is sown by the wayside.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now it is not the same thing for the doctrine to wither away, when no man is evil entreating, or disturbing its foundations, as when temptations press upon one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they that are likened to the thorns, are much more inexcusable than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order then that none of these things may befall us, let us by zeal and continual remembrance cover up the things that are told us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though the devil do catch them away, yet it rests with us, whether they be caught away; though the plants wither, yet it is not from the heat this takes place (for He did not say, because of the heat it withered, but, “because it had no root”); although His sayings are choked, it is not because of the thorns, but of them who suffer them to spring up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is a way, if thou wilt, to check this evil growth, and to make the right use of our wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, “the world,” but “the care of the world;” nor “riches,” but “the deceitfulness of riches.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then blame the things, but the corrupt mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is possible to be rich and not to be deceived; and to be in this world, and not to be choked with its cares.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed riches have two contrary disadvantages; one, care, wearing us out, and bringing a darkness over us; the other, luxury, making us effeminate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And well hath He said, “The deceitfulness of riches.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all that pertains to riches is deceit; they are names only, not attached to things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so pleasure and glory, and splendid array, and all these things, are a sort of vain show, not a reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having therefore spoken of the ways of destruction, afterwards He mentions the good ground, not suffering them to despair, but giving a hope of repentance, and indicating that it is possible to change from the things before mentioned into this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet if both the land be good, and the Sower one, and the seed the same, wherefore did one bear a hundred, one sixty, one thirty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here again the difference is from the nature of the ground, for even where the ground is good, great even therein is the difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, that not the husbandman is to be blamed, nor the seed, but the land that receives it? not for its nature, but for its disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And herein too, great is His mercy to man, that He doth not require one measure of virtue, but while He receives the first, and casts not out the second, He gives also a place to the third.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things He saith, least they that followed Him should suppose that hearing is sufficient for salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore, one may say, did He not put the other vices also, such as lust, vainglory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In speaking of “the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches,” He set down all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, both vainglory and all the rest belong to this world, and to the deceitfulness of riches; such as pleasure, and gluttony, and envy, and vainglory, and all the like.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He added also the “way” and the “rock,” signifying that it is not enough to be freed from riches only, but we must cultivate also the other parts of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what if thou art free indeed from riches, yet are soft and unmanly? and what if thou art not indeed unmanly, but art remiss and careless about the hearing of the word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, no one part is sufficient for our salvation, but there is required first a careful hearing, and a continual recollection; then fortitude, then contempt of riches, and deliverance from all worldly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In fact, His reason for putting this before the other, is because the one is first required (for “How shall they believe except they hear?”just as we too, except we mind what is said, shall not be able so much as to learn what we ought to do): after that, fortitude, and the contempt of things present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hearing therefore these things, let us fortify ourselves on all sides, regarding His instructions, and striking our roots deep, and cleansing ourselves from all worldly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if we do the one, neglecting the other, we shall be nothing bettered; for though we perish not in one way, yet shall we in some other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what signifies our not being ruined by riches, if we are by indolence: or not by indolence, if we are by softness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so the husbandman, whether this way or that way he lose his crop, equally bewails himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then soothe ourselves upon our not perishing in all these ways, but let it be our grief, in whichever way we are perishing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let us burn up the thorns, for they choke the word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is known to those rich men, who not for these matters alone, but for others also prove unprofitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having become slaves and captives of their pleasures, they are useless even for civil affairs, and if for them, much more for those of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and in two ways hereby our thoughts are corrupted; both by the luxury, and by the anxiety too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For either of these by itself were enough to overwhelm the bark; but when even both concur, imagine how high the billow swells.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And marvel not at His calling our luxury, “thorns.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou indeed art not aware of it, being intoxicated with thy passion, but they that are in sound health know that it pricks sharper than any thorn, and that luxury wastes the soul worse than care, and causes more grievous pains both to body and soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one is not so sorely smitten by anxiety, as by surfeiting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since when watchings, and throbbings of the temples, and heaviness in the head, and pangs of the bowels, lay hold of such a man, you may imagine how many thorns these surpass in grievousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as the thorns, on whichever side they are laid hold of, draw blood from the hands that seize them, just so doth luxury plague both feet, and hands, and head, and eyes, and in general all our members; and it is withered also, and unfruitful, like the thorn, and hurts much more than it, and in our vital parts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, it brings on premature old age, and dulls the senses, and darkens our reasoning, and blinds the keen-sighted mind, and makes the body tumid,rendering excessive the deposition of that which is cast away, and gathering together a great accumulation of evils; and it makes the burden too great, and the load overwhelming; whence our falls are many and continual, and our shipwrecks frequent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, why pamper thy body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? are we to slay thee in sacrifice, to set thee on the table?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The birds it is well for thee to pamper: or rather, not so well even for them; for when they are fattened, they are unprofitable for wholesome food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great an evil is luxury, that its mischief is shown even in irrational beings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even them by luxury we make unprofitable, both to themselves and to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For their superfluous flesh is indigestible, and the moister kind of corruption is engendered by that kind of fatness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereas the creatures that are not so fed, but live, as one may say, in abstinence, and moderate diet, and in labor and hardship, these are most serviceable both to themselves and to others, as well for food, as for everything else.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Those, at any rate, who live on them, are in better health; but such as are fed on the others are like them, growing dull and sickly, and rendering their chain more grievous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing is so hostile and hurtful to the body, as luxury; nothing so tears it in pieces, and overloads and corrupts it, as intemperance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore above all may this circumstance make one amazed at them for their folly, that not even so much care as others show towards their wine skins, are these willing to evince towards themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For those the wine merchants do not allow to receive more than is fit, lest they should burst; but to their own wretched belly these men do not vouchsafe even so much forethought, but when they have stuffed it and distended it, they fill all, up to the ears, up to the nostrils, to the very throat itself, thereby pressing into half its room the spirit, and the power that directs the living being.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? was thy throat given thee for this end, that thou shouldest fill it up to the very mouth, with wine turned sour, and all other corruption?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not for this, O man, but that thou shouldest above all things sing to God, and offer up the holy prayers, and read out the divine laws, and give to thy neighbors profitable counsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, as if thou hadst received it for this end, dost not suffer it to have leisure for that ministry, so much as for a short season, but for all thy life subjectest it to this evil slavery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as if any man having had a lyre given him with golden strings, and beautifully constructed, instead of awakening with it the most harmonious music, were to cover it over with much dung and clay; even so do these men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now the word, dung, I use not of living, but of luxurious living, and of that great wantonness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because what is more than necessary is not nourishment, but merely injurious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth the belly alone was made merely for the reception of food; but the month, and the throat, and tongue, for other things also, far more necessary than these: or rather, not even the belly for the reception of food simply, but for the reception of moderate food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this it makes manifest by crying out loudly against us, when we tease it by this greediness; nor doth it clamor against us only, but also avenging that wrong exacts of us the severest penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And first it punishes the feet, that bear and conduct us to those wicked revels, then the hands that minister to it, binding them together for having brought unto it such quantities and kinds of provisions; and many have distorted even their very mouth, and eyes, and head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as a servant receiving an order beyond his power, not seldom out of desperation becomes insolent to the giver of the order: so the belly too, together with these members, often ruins and destroys, from being over-strained, the very brain itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this God hath well ordered, that from excess so much mischief should arise; that when of thine own will thou dost not practise self-restraint, at least against thy will, for fear of so great ruin, thou mayest learn to be moderate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing then these things, let us flee luxury, let us study moderation, that we may both enjoy health of body, and having delivered our soul from all infirmity, may attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
